summary: electron-orbit ships an obfuscated TLS exfiltration client and stages process.env at postinstall under a borrowed OrbitDB identity
details:
electron-orbit@1.0.10 is published under the name and author of the OrbitDB Archive's orbit-electron project but does not implement that functionality. Two installer-harm mechanisms are present:

1. Postinstall env staging (install.js, run via package.json scripts.postinstall="node install.js"): on `npm install`, the script dumps the installer's process.env (filtering keys containing 'path') along with cwd and hostname/username to /tmp/electron_orbit_install_marker.txt. The access to globalThis.process.env is constructed through an XOR string deobfuscator to hide the env-dump from static review.

2. Obfuscated TLS exfiltration client on import (main module): the package ships a from-scratch TLS 1.3 implementation (AEAD/HKDF/ECDH handshake) with all key strings — 'node:net', 'connect', ALPN 'http/1.1', and the HTTP request line/headers — assembled at runtime via the same XOR deobfuscator. On require(), setupAutoRenderOnImport() calls retrieveIcon(), which dynamically imports node:net, opens a raw TCP socket to port 443 on a host taken from registeredIconSources, and issues an HTTP GET via the custom TLS stack (bypassing standard https/TLS interception). The destination host is selected by an environment-keyed gate: registeredIconSources.orbit is populated only when sha256(process.env.BuildType) is a substring of a hardcoded 64-hex-char value; otherwise the URL defaults to a non-functional '%TEMP%' placeholder. This SHA256-gated activation keeps the live exfil URL dormant on scanners and most developer machines and fires only in the attacker-known environment.

The combination — hand-rolled TLS client + XOR-obfuscated networking/process.env access + hash-gated payload activation + postinstall env dump + borrowed OSS identity that doesn't match the actual code — is engineered supply-chain malware, not a legitimate Electron/OrbitDB tool.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
